impact: The daemon can stop retrying the same broad extension lifecycle skeleton request after repeated failures and resume with deterministic, one-cycle tasks: first a source-evidence map, then a small JSON-only extension fixture, then validation. This reduces syntax and scope risk while preserving completed work and keeping live/authenticated automation out of scope.
summary: Narrow the failed lifecycle skeleton work into fixture-first recovery tasks and park the stuck active task.
target_task: Supervisor repair: 3 consecutive daemon rounds failed without an accepted patch

command: files=$(find ppd -name '*.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print); test -z "$files" || npx tsc --noEmit --target ES2020 --module ESNext --moduleResolution node --strict --skipLibCheck --types node $files
